title: sempervivum hirtum subsp. patens
intro: Sempervivum hirtum subsp. patens is a species of the succulent plant, commonly known as the woolly houseleek, which is a type of evergreen rosette-forming plant that belongs to the family Crassulaceae. The medicinal properties of Sempervivum hirtum subsp. patens include its ability to act as an antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory agent, which can be used to treat various health conditions such as wounds, burns, and skin infections. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and even exhibit antioxidant properties. Sempervivum hirtum subsp. patens can be prepared into herbal remedies such as tinctures, salves, or infusions, which can be used topically to reap its medicinal benefits.
